executive_brief: CourseHub is a REST API for an online learning platform enabling instructors to create and manage sequenced courses and students to enroll and track learning progress. The system implements a shared user identity model with role-based access control to isolate ownership and enrollment data. Core value is delivered through secure content publishing and a monitored student journey from registration to 100% completion.

architectural_constraints: Routing: All endpoints must be exposed under /api/v1/
Response Format: Mandatory JSON envelope with { "data": ..., "meta": ..., "errors": [] }
Data Validation: Course prices must be stored strictly in cents
Data Validation: Enrollment progress values must be between 0 and 100 inclusive
QA Gate: Minimum 90% coverage of core course, enrollment, and authorization behaviors by automated E2E tests
Isolation: Instructors are restricted to managing only courses they own
Isolation: Students are restricted to viewing/updating only their own enrollments
Workflow: Enrollment is strictly permitted only for courses with a 'published' flag set to true
Ordering: Modules within a course must maintain a fixed sequence

maturity_assessment: The specification is logically robust and conceptually complete, with all core functional requirements and edge cases mapped to success criteria. While there are minor structural gaps regarding explicit out-of-scope boundaries and minor technical uncertainties, these do not impede the core implementation logic. The project is READY for execution.
critical_dependencies: RESEND_API_KEY environment variable for registration welcome emails
Referential integrity: Course deletion must be blocked if active Enrollment entities exist
Relational dependency: Enrollment requires valid foreign keys to both User and Course entities
Role-based access control: Shared User table must correctly resolve 'instructor' vs 'student' roles for all authorization gates
